reasoning: To reach 15 bcm in a single quarter in 2025, the EU would have to significantly exceed its 2024 quarterly run-rate of roughly 13.5 bcm. However, the suspension of the Ukraine pipeline transit in January 2025 removed approximately 16 bcm/year (4 bcm/quarter) of import capacity. Remaining active avenues for Russian gas are primarily limited to LNG and the European string of TurkStream. In 2024, LNG accounted for roughly 5 bcm/quarter and TurkStream was running near its capacity of around 4-4.5 bcm/quarter to the EU. Combined, this caps the theoretical maximum imports from Russia at around 9-10 bcm/quarter going forward unless the Ukraine pipeline restarts or massive new LNG capacity is magically brought online on short notice. Given the lack of both, breaking the 15 bcm threshold in any quarter of 2025 is physically virtually impossible.
